methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
Viken Detection is a Series C stage company in the Defense & Security sector, having raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 12 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, spanning 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 10 private entities including Helsing ($18.0B Series D), Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Quantum-Systems ($8.0B Series C), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), and Dream Security ($3.0B Series B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $85M. The peer-median method references the median valuation across the 12-company comparable set at $1.8B, with a range spanning $300M to $18.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in maturity, technology differentiation, and revenue traction within the defense tech category.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $72M, a median of $930M, and a high of $2.0B. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty around Viken's relative positioning within the peer cohort. Key variables affecting valuation include actual revenue and deployment scale relative to comparable companies, differentiation of detection technology, and customer concentration risk in what remains a concentrated buyer universe.
